title: Relative Symplectic Cohomology of Pairs
speaker: Adi Dickstein
abstract: Relative symplectic cohomology, an invariant of subsets in a symplectic manifold, was recently introduced by Varolgunes. In this talk, I will present a generalization of this invariant to pairs of subsets, which shares similar properties with the singular cohomology of pairs, such as excision and a product structure. Using this new invariant, I will demonstrate new symplectic rigidity phenomena. Joint with Yaniv Ganor, Leonid Polterovich and Frol Zapolsky.

proof_stages.label: singular cohomology template for pairs
proof_stages.summary: The talk begins by recalling singular cohomology at the chain level, defining cohomology of a pair as a kernel of restriction, and emphasizing the Mayer--Vietoris/excision and product structures that the symplectic theory will imitate.

proof_stages.label: relative symplectic cohomology chain model and homotopy kernel
proof_stages.summary: Varolgunes's relative symplectic cohomology of compact subsets is recalled via monotone Hamiltonians, Floer cochain direct limits and completion, and the homotopy-coherent restriction maps motivate defining the pair theory by the cocone of a restriction map.

proof_stages.label: descent, Mayer--Vietoris, and products for pairs
proof_stages.summary: The speaker introduces descent as the symplectic analogue of excision, notes that Poisson-commuting sets provide a usable sufficient condition, and states the relative product for pairs together with its upgrade from intersections to unions under descent.

proof_stages.label: ideal-valued quasi-measure from relative symplectic cohomology
proof_stages.summary: An ideal-valued quasi-measure is defined by axioms including monotonicity, quasi-multiplicativity for commuting sets, invariance and vanishing, then constructed from relative symplectic cohomology by kernels of restrictions to complements, with quasi-multiplicativity proved using the exact triangle and pair product.

proof_stages.label: computations of the quasi-measure ideals
proof_stages.summary: The quantum-cohomology-valued quasi-measure is computed for standard isotropic coordinate tori in T^{2n} and for product situations involving equators in S^2, producing explicit nonzero ideals used in later rigidity arguments.

proof_stages.label: non-displaceability from nonzero ideal products
proof_stages.summary: A proposition shows that if the product of the quasi-measure ideals of two compact sets is nonzero, then no element of Symp_0 can displace one from the other, and the computed torus/S^2 ideals yield new non-displaceability results for product Lagrangians.

proof_stages.label: center-point theorem via involutive maps
proof_stages.summary: Gromov's ideal-valued measures and their center-point theorem are reviewed, involutive maps are used to push an IVQM forward to an IVM, and the resulting theorem is applied to families of subsets in T^6 x S^2 whose ideals have nonzero third power.

proof_stages.label: virtual commuting and obstructions
proof_stages.summary: The final topic asks when two sets can be made commuting by a symplectomorphism, defines virtual commuting, proves an elementary obstruction for closed connected isotropic submanifolds, and applies topological and IVQM non-displacement arguments to produce non-virtually-commuting pairs.
